queries.query: What color was the hat left behind?

standardTrace.finalAnswer: The hat left behind was blue.

steps.name: Query Embedding
steps.description: Converted the query into a dense vector (text-embedding-3-small).

steps.name: Vector Resemblance Search
steps.description: Computed cosine similarity across all text chunks.

chunks.text: Sherlock Holmes sat in his armchair, observing the blue hat that had been left behind. The hat belonged to Mr. Henry Baker.

graphTrace.finalAnswer: The hat was blue. It belonged to Henry Baker.

steps.name: Node Matching & Expansion
steps.description: Found 'Blue Hat' node. Traversed 1 hop to find 'Henry Baker' and 'Sherlock Holmes'.

steps.name: Community Report Retrieval
steps.description: Failed to find specific community report for purely local node query. Relying on raw nodes for prompt.

dataPayload.warning: Over-retrieval possible. Local queries do not benefit fully from GraphRAG summaries.

queries.query: What are the recurring themes across cases?

steps.name: Vector Search
steps.description: Searching for similar embeddings to 'recurring themes across cases'

steps.name: Retrieved Scattershot Chunks
steps.description: Retrieved chunks mentioning 'cases' or 'themes', but missing holistic context.

chunks.text: Watson noted that Holmes often relied on seemingly insignificant details, a theme across many of their cases involving stolen items.

graphTrace.finalAnswer: Across cases, a recurring theme is the significance of seemingly mundane observations. Holmes routinely uses small clues, like a forgotten blue hat, to deduce larger patterns about the crimes and human nature.

steps.name: Community Report Retrieval
steps.description: Global query detected. Bypassing raw node matching and retrieving top-level community summaries.

reports.title: The Mystery of the Blue Hat
reports.summary: Sherlock Holmes and John Watson are investigating a seemingly mundane blue hat that belongs to Mr. Henry Baker. Watson observes that Holmes uses these small clues to deduce larger themes of stolen items.
